pages.full_text:
of the houses. We looked at the river (v. smelly) & Squares & went back to St. Bavo's to see the Altarpiece of the Adoration of the Lamb by Hubert & Jan van Eyck. 1432. V. crowded. Jewel-like colours. V. impressive.
	We shopped at a toy shop & bread shop. And we each chose 100 BFr. (£2) of little chocolates in our own bags. Tea in the car park & we set off at 5.40.
pages.notes: Page continues mid-sentence from a previous page — text begins 'of the houses.' No handwritten page number visible in the top-left corner. Contains a glued-in rubber-stamp or printed shop logo for 'Sweertvaegher' (a chocolate shop in Ghent) in purple/violet ink, with handwritten caption 'Chocolate shop.' below it. The Altarpiece of the Adoration of the Lamb by Hubert & Jan van Eyck (1432) is in St. Bavo's Cathedral, Ghent, Belgium. '100 BFr.' refers to 100 Belgian Francs. 'Bavo's' spelled as written — could also be read as 'Bavors' but 'Bavo's' is the correct Ghent cathedral and matches the letter forms.

pages.full_text: Just Judges and Knights of Christ
photo_captions: Just Judges and Knights of Christ
pages.notes: Page is almost entirely occupied by a glued-in colour photograph or postcard showing two panels from the Ghent Altarpiece (van Eyck): the 'Just Judges' (left panel) and 'Knights of Christ' (right panel). The handwritten caption below reads 'Just Judges and Knights of Christ'. A faint number is visible in the top-right corner but is not clearly legible — reported as null. The gutter overlap carries text from the left page ('& t', 'th', '!', ') of', 'the') which belongs to the left page and is not transcribed here.
